root_text.sn49.23-34:1.1: “Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, ye keci balakaraṇīyā kammantā kayiranti, sabbe te pathaviṁ nissāya pathaviyaṁ patiṭṭhāya evamete balakaraṇīyā kammantā kayiranti;
root_text.sn49.23-34:1.2: evameva kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya cattāro sammappadhāne bhāveti, cattāro sammappadhāne bahulīkaroti.
root_text.sn49.23-34:1.3: Kathañca, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya cattāro sammappadhāne bhāveti, cattāro sammappadhāne bahulīkaroti?
root_text.sn49.23-34:1.4: Idha, bhikkhave, bhikkhu anuppannānaṁ pāpakānaṁ akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ anuppādāya chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati …pe…
root_text.sn49.23-34:1.5: uppannānaṁ kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ ṭhitiyā asammosāya bhiyyobhāvāya vepullāya bhāvanāya pāripūriyā chandaṁ janeti vāyamati vīriyaṁ ārabhati cittaṁ paggaṇhāti padahati.
root_text.sn49.23-34:1.6: Evaṁ kho, bhikkhave, bhikkhu sīlaṁ nissāya sīle patiṭṭhāya cattāro sammappadhāne bhāveti, cattāro sammappadhāne bahulīkarotī”ti.

translation_text.sn49.23-34:1.1: “Mendicants, all the hard work that gets done depends on the earth and is grounded on the earth.
translation_text.sn49.23-34:1.2: In the same way, a mendicant develops and cultivates the four right efforts depending on and grounded on ethics.
translation_text.sn49.23-34:1.4: It’s when a mendicant generates enthusiasm, tries, makes an effort, exerts the mind, and strives so that bad, unskillful qualities don’t arise. …
translation_text.sn49.23-34:1.5: so that skillful qualities that have arisen remain, are not lost, but increase, mature, and are completed by development.
translation_text.sn49.23-34:1.6: That’s how a mendicant develops and cultivates the four right efforts depending on and grounded on ethics.”
translation_text.sn49.23-34:2.2: a tree, a pot, and a spike,
translation_text.sn49.23-34:2.3: the atmosphere, and two on clouds,
translation_text.sn49.23-34:2.4: a ship, a guest house, and a river.
